interviewStages: Behavioral: customer focus, ownership, navigating mature SaaS
codingTopics: OO design: model a business domain such as workflows with triggers and actions
Multi-tenant data structures: per-tenant rate limiting, isolation
Streaming: rolling aggregates over tenant events
Graph and DAG: workflow evaluation, dependency resolution
Search and filters: AND/OR queries over records
systemDesignTopics: Design a multi-channel ticket routing system (email, chat, voice, social)
Design SLA tracking with timezone-aware deadlines

coding: At Zendesk, model a workflow engine with triggers, conditions, and actions. Evaluate a workflow DAG and detect cycles.
Implement a per-tenant rate limiter so one customer cannot exhaust shared capacity. Discuss the token-bucket design.
Given a stream of tenant events, maintain rolling per-entity scores with bounded memory.
Implement a filter query over records supporting AND/OR on custom fields. Discuss indexing for a multi-tenant store.
Deduplicate records via fuzzy clustering on name and email. Discuss the matching and complexity.
behavioral: Tell me about a project you are proud of at Zendesk scale and the hardest tradeoff you made.
Give me an example that shows customer focus, ownership, navigating mature saas.
Describe a time you disagreed with a teammate or manager. How did it resolve?
Tell me about a time you took ownership of an ambiguous problem end to end.
systemDesign: Design a multi-channel ticket routing system (email, chat, voice, social).
Design SLA tracking with timezone-aware deadlines.
values: Zendesk succeeds when its customers do. Describe a time you solved for the customer even at a cost to the team.
Tell me about a time you handled a tricky multi-tenant isolation or permissions problem.
How do you balance enterprise customization against product simplicity?
other: How does Zendesk compare to Intercom or Freshworks?
How has private ownership changed engineering?

Zendesk Interview Guide (2026): Customer Service Platform

				techinterview.org			
			
		

		 /  May 5, 2026 
			
		

	
	
	

		
		
On this page

- Process

- What they actually ask

- Prep priorities

Updated May 5, 2026 · techinterview.org

⏱ 1 min read

Zendesk
zendesk.com ↗

Zendesk is the dominant customer service platform — used by 100K+ companies including Uber, Airbnb, and Slack. Taken private by Hellman & Friedman in 2022. The interview emphasizes ticket management, multi-channel routing, and the realities of running a platform that supports billions of customer interactions yearly.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute coding phone (DSA medium) → onsite virtual: 2 coding, 1 system design, 1 craft deep-dive, 1 behavioral. Cycle: 3–4 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design a multi-channel ticket routing system (email, chat, voice, social)

- Design SLA tracking with timezone-aware deadlines

- Design Zendesk AI — assistant for support agents, ticket routing, summarization

- Coding: medium DSA, often with state-machine or graph framing

- Behavioral: customer focus, ownership, navigating mature SaaS

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE II: $145K–$180K total

- Senior SE: $210K–$280K

- Staff: $300K–$400K

- Principal: $420K–$560K

Prep priorities

- Be fluent in Ruby on Rails (the bulk of the codebase)

- Understand multi-tenant SaaS patterns and SOC2 compliance basics

- Read about Zendesk's engineering blog and its multi-channel architecture

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Zendesk remote-friendly?

Hybrid in San Francisco (HQ), Madison WI, Dublin, Melbourne. Some engineering roles fully remote within supported countries.

How does Zendesk compare to Intercom or Freshworks?

Zendesk has the deepest enterprise traction. Intercom is more product-led. Freshworks competes on price. Zendesk pays comparably to Intercom.

How has private ownership changed engineering?

More focus on profitability, less expansive growth. Stable but less aggressive in hiring than peak-IPO era.
